bid_packages.project_name: San Marcos Aquatic Resources Center (ARC) - Rehabilitate Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building
bid_packages.summary: This document is a solicitation for a Firm Fixed Price contract by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service for the rehabilitation of a shop and garage building at the San Marcos Aquatic Resources Center. It includes requirements for demolition, installation of garage doors, lighting replacement, and roofing work.

bid_packages.project_location: Padre Island National Seashore, Corpus Christi, Texas
bid_packages.summary: Request for Quote for Cabin Security and Improvements at Padre Island National Seashore in Corpus Christi, Texas. The project involves repair and improvement work including roof repairs, door reinforcement, and installation of hurricane shutters.

bid_packages.project_name: Cabin Security and Improvements at Padre Island National Seashore
bid_packages.project_location: Padre Island National Seashore, Corpus Christi, Texas
bid_packages.summary: [amendment of solicitation] This document is an amendment to the solicitation for the Cabin Security and Improvements project at Padre Island National Seashore. It includes changes to the site visit date due to inclement weather and extends the quote due date. The project involves commercial and institutional building construction with a performance period of 60 days from the Notice to Proceed. Key facts: NAICS: 236220; Small Business Size Standard: $45m; Bonding: Payment Bond or Irrevocable Letter of Credit (100% of contract value) due within 10 days after award Key dates: Site visit: 6/4/2026 at 10am CT; Quote due date: 6/18/2026 at 12pm CT

bid_packages.project_location: Cleburne High School, 850 N. Nolan River Road, Cleburne, Texas 76033
bid_packages.summary: Cleburne Independent School District is accepting competitive sealed proposals for the renovation of cafeteria serving lines at Cleburne High School. Proposals are due by 2:00 PM on June 8, 2026.

bid_packages.project_location: College Station, TX, Harrington Education Center Office Tower, 0435
bid_packages.summary: Notice of Project for the Harrington Education Center Lab 303 Renovation at Texas A&M University. Includes project details and submission information for the CSP RFP & HSP.

bid_packages.project_name: San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building Renovation 2026
bid_packages.project_location: 500 East McCarty Lane, San Marcos, TX 78666-1024
bid_packages.owner: United States Fish and Wildlife Service
inclusions: Replace the three overhead roll-up style doors to include hardware, weather stripping and manual chain hoists.
Replace current light fixtures with LED light fixtures to meet OSHA requirements.
Remove all suspended ceiling grids and tiles. Approximately 416 square feet.
Replace three (3) exterior personnel doors with insulated hollow metal doors to include frames and hardware.
Terminate, cap and remove existing gas lines from the building.
Remove and replace existing gutter system including down spouts.
Remove and replace existing roof with new 24-gauge roofing panels. Exclude lean-to shed (awning) on the south side of the building.
Remove existing gas fired unit heaters.
bid_packages.summary: This document outlines the scope of work for the renovation of the San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center's shop and 2 stall garage building. The renovation includes replacing doors, light fixtures, ceiling tiles, and the roof, as well as removing gas lines and heaters. The project is managed by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

bid_packages.pdf_name: 26-007 RFCSP Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation.pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a solicitation notice for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes complete demolition and replacement of interior finishes, mechanical, plumbing, electrical systems, and new IT infrastructure.

[other] This document is a Department of Labor Wage Determination, which outlines the prevailing wage rates for various labor categories in a specific geographic area. It is a standard document used to ensure compliance with federal wage laws on government contracts.

bid_packages.pdf_name: 26-007 RFCSP Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation (1).pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a supporting document related to the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes various attachments such as prevailing wage rates, tax exemption certificate, and general conditions.

bid_packages.pdf_name: 26-007 RFCSP Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation (2).pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a supporting document related to the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes attachments such as prevailing wage rates, tax exemption certificate, and general conditions.

[other] This document appears to be related to the Davis-Bacon Act wage determinations for Hays County. It likely contains information about prevailing wage rates for construction projects in the area, as mandated by the Davis-Bacon Act. The document is primarily boilerplate and does not provide specific project details.

[other] This document is a Texas Sales and Use Tax Exemption Certification form. It allows the purchaser to claim an exemption from sales and use taxes for certain taxable items. The form includes fields for the purchaser's and seller's information and requires the purchaser to acknowledge the legal implications of misuse.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1779308778671_26-007 Carr EFA Dressing Room Addendum #1.pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is an addendum to the Request for Competitive Sealed Proposals for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation at Angelo State University. It includes project information, schedule, proposal requirements, and evaluation criteria.

bid_packages.project_name: San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building

unit_prices.description: Remove and replace the three overhead sectional doors to include hardware, weather stripping and openers.
Remove and replace existing light fixtures with LED light fixtures to meet OSHA requirements.
Remove all suspended ceiling grids and tiles.
Remove and replace the existing three exterior personnel doors with insulated hollow metal doors to include frames, hardware, weather stripping and threshold.
Terminate, cap and remove existing gas lines from the building.
Remove and replace existing gutter system to include and replace down spouts.
Remove and replace existing roof with new roofing sheets.
Remove existing gas fired unit heaters.

bid_packages.summary: This bid schedule requests pricing for various construction tasks including the removal and replacement of doors, light fixtures, ceiling grids, gas lines, gutter systems, roofing sheets, and unit heaters at the San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880767322_26-007 Carr EFA Dressing Room Renocation RFCSP.pdf
bid_packages.summary: The document is a Request for Competitive Sealed Proposals for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. The project involves demolition and replacement of interior finishes and systems, with a construction budget of $500,000. Proposals are due by June 5, 2026.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880829538_26-007 Carr EFA Dressing Room Renocation Attachment A.pdf

unit_prices.description: Include a cash/contingency allowance for unforeseen scope of work.

bid_packages.summary: The bid form requests proposals for construction services for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project. It includes a base proposal for construction services, a specified allowance for unforeseen work, and certifications and affirmations required by Texas law.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880857161_Attachment B.2 - Sample Construction Services Agreement.pdf
bid_packages.summary: [sample construction services agreement] This document is a sample construction services agreement for a project at Angelo State University. It outlines the scope of work, contract sum, time of commencement and completion, HUB contracting commitments, liquidated damages, dispute resolution, indemnity, and various miscellaneous provisions. The agreement emphasizes the use of certified HUB contractors for at least 30% of the total contract sum and details the procedures for handling changes to the HUB Subcontracting Plan. Key facts: HUB contracting commitment: 30% of total contract sum; Liquidated damages: $###.00 per calendar day

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_518571_1779300513068_RFCSP 2026-0608-01 CHS Cafeteria Serving Line Renovation.pdf
bid_packages.project_location: Cleburne High School, 850 N. Nolan River Road, Cleburne, Texas 76033
inclusions: The Contractor shall complete the removal and disposal of existing serving lines and installation of new serving lines.
The Contractor shall be able to complete all tasks normally encountered in commercial kitchen equipment replacement.
The Contractor must comply with all National, State and Local codes and pay all applicable costs, fees and permits.
Prices must include all transportation costs.
All new equipment are turn-key purchases and must include all needed installations, equipment and related costs.
bid_packages.summary: Cleburne ISD is soliciting proposals for the renovation of cafeteria serving lines at Cleburne High School. The project includes removal and disposal of existing equipment, procurement and installation of new equipment, with a budget of $295,000. Proposals are due by June 8, 2026.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880917414_Attachment F.1 - Tom Green County Prevailing Wage 2023 (TTUS).pdf
bid_packages.summary: [other] This document is a prevailing wage determination for Tom Green County, specifically for a building construction project at Angelo State University in San Angelo. It lists the prevailing wage rates for various trade classifications as of July 2023. Contractors are responsible for classifying workers according to these rates. Key facts: Construction type: Building; Location: Tom Green County; Date: July 2023

bid_packages.project_name: Alter Command Post and Nondestructive Inspection (NDI) Room
bid_packages.project_location: 136th Airlift Wing, Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base, Fort Worth, TX
bid_packages.owner: 136th Airlift Wing, Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base, Fort Worth, TX
inclusions: Furnish all labor, materials, equipment, supervision, and services necessary to renovate the designated spaces into complete, durable, and fully operational facilities.
Perform all required interior wall demolition and construction, painting, caulking, carpentry, and associated electrical, mechanical, and plumbing work to deliver the finished spaces.

Ensure all work complies strictly with applicable federal, state, and local codes, as well as all DoD standards, including the Unified Facilities Criteria (UFCs) and Unified Facilities Guide Specifications (UFGSs) cited in any project documentation.
Provide any and all components, services, or materials required to deliver a complete and fully functional system, even if not explicitly specified in this SOW.
Protect all adjacent vehicles, equipment, landscaping, and facilities from overspray, debris, and damage.
Install and maintain a clearly marked construction boundary fence to identify the limits of the work area.
Comply with all applicable Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Army Corps of Engineers EM 385‑1‑1, and State safety requirements.
Maintain a clean job site at all times. Demolition debris and excess materials shall be removed daily.
Coordinate inspections with the Contracting Officer’s Representative (COR) prior to commencing and during critical phases of work.
Request a Pre-Final Inspection at approximately 75% completion to identify deficiencies requiring correction prior to final completion.
Request a Final Acceptance Inspection at 100% completion, submitted at least 14 days in advance and include written assurance that all deficiencies have been corrected.
Provide proof of insurance.
Provide electronic submittals on the AF 3000 Material Approval Submittal form for each item listed on the AF 66 Submittal Register, as applicable.
Provide a submittal register, updated at least twice per month.
Provide bi-weekly meeting minutes for each progress meeting, updated schedule by task, planned work for the next week.
Provide a Letter of Guarantee that warrants the project against defects in materials and workmanship for one year from the date of final acceptance.
Provide minimum one (1) year on all workmanship and standard manufacturer’s warranty on products.
Electrical power and water shall be provided as required. The Contractor is responsible for any required connections.
Obtain and maintain all necessary base access for its personnel, including subcontractors.
Halt any ground disturbance activity if any Native American or other historical artifacts, fossils, human remains, unmarked graves, or burial sites are found.
bid_packages.summary: This document outlines the scope of work for the renovation of the Command Post and Nondestructive Inspection (NDI) Room at the 136th Airlift Wing, Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base, Fort Worth, TX. The project involves interior demolition, construction, and compliance with federal, state, and DoD standards. The contractor is responsible for a complete, turnkey renovation, including all necessary labor, materials, and services.

[general instructions to bidders] This document provides detailed construction instructions for alterations involving metal stud framing, gypsum board installation, painting, and flooring to match existing finishes. It includes specifications for electrical, HVAC, and security installations, such as mantrap doors and emergency systems. The document outlines requirements for ceiling modifications, door installations, and the relocation of existing equipment to accommodate new wall partitions. Key facts: Project Number: DDPM262001; Install VCT: AZrock, Shale Gray #V-866; Install new carpet: Shaw Contract, Clear Tile Style# 59564, color Moonstone 62500; Paint standard color: Sherwin-Williams SW1016 Silver Screen

bid_packages.pdf_name: Solicitation - W50S7626QA001 Cmd Post and NDI.pdf
bid_packages.project_name: Renovate Space for Command Post and Nondestructive Inspection (NDI) Operations
inclusions: ASBESTOS WORKER/HEAT & FROST INSULATOR: $33.23/hr + $7.52 fringe
BOILERMAKER: $33.17/hr + $24.92 fringe
MILLWRIGHT: $32.02/hr + $11.27 fringe
ELEVATOR MECHANIC: $51.93/hr + $38.435 fringe
POWER EQUIPMENT OPERATOR (Tower Crane): $32.85/hr + $13.10 fringe
IRONWORKER (ORNAMENTAL AND STRUCTURAL): $29.64/hr + $8.43 fringe
PAINTER (Brush, Roller, and Spray): $16.40/hr + $5.45 fringe
PIPEFITTER (Excludes HVAC Pipe Installation): $41.28/hr + $12.16 fringe
bid_packages.summary: This document is a Davis-Bacon wage determination for Tarrant County, Texas, effective 01/02/2026. It includes prevailing wage rates for various construction trades involved in building projects.

[amendment of solicitation] This document is an amendment to the solicitation W50S7626QA001, specifically incorporating revision 1 to the B1672 (CLIN 0001) plans. It includes modifications to contract clauses, particularly regarding contract drawings and specifications. The amendment requires acknowledgment from offerors and outlines responsibilities for checking and reproducing drawings. Key facts: Project Number: DDPM262101; Location: Fort Worth, TX Key dates: 03 May 2026; 13 May 2026

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880887164_Attachment D - HSP (Historically Underutilized Businesses (HUB) Subcontracting Plan) documents.pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a HUB Subcontracting Plan (HSP) required by the State of Texas for contracts with probable subcontracting opportunities. It outlines the requirements for respondents to complete and submit the HSP with their bid, including sections on subcontracting intentions, self-performing justification, and good faith effort methods. The plan aims to promote equal business opportunities for economically disadvantaged persons by encouraging the use of Texas certified Historically Underutilized Businesses (HUBs).

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880869361_Attachment C - 2010 Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions dated (TTUS edited 09.07.2023).pdf
bid_packages.summary: [general instructions to bidders] This document outlines the Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions for construction contracts under the Texas Tech University System. It incorporates specific supplementary conditions applicable to all Texas Tech System and member institution construction projects, alongside the Uniform General Conditions from the Texas Facilities Commission. The document includes detailed articles on definitions, wage rates, responsibilities, subcontracting plans, bonds, insurance, construction documents, safety, quality control, schedules, payments, changes, project completion, warranties, suspension, termination, and dispute resolution. Key facts: Document revision date: 09/07/2023; Incorporates Texas Facilities Commission's 2010 edition Uniform General Conditions

sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880887164_Attachment D -: This document is a HUB Subcontracting Plan (HSP) required by the State of Texas for contracts with probable subcontracting opportunities. It outlines the requirements for respondents to complete and submit the HSP with their bid, including sections on subcontracting intentions, self-performing justification, and good faith effort methods. The plan aims to promote equal business opportunities for economically disadvantaged persons by encouraging the use of Texas certified Historically Underutilized Businesses (HUBs).
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880869361_Attachment C -: [general instructions to bidders] This document outlines the Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions for construction contracts under the Texas Tech University System. It incorporates specific supplementary conditions applicable to all Texas Tech System and member institution construction projects, alongside the Uniform General Conditions from the Texas Facilities Commission. The document includes detailed articles on definitions, wage rates, responsibilities, subcontracting plans, bonds, insurance, construction documents, safety, quality control, schedules, payments, changes, project completion, warranties, suspension, termination, and dispute resolution. Key facts: Document revision date: 09/07/2023; Incorporates Texas Facilities Commission's 2010 edition Uniform General Conditions
